# The Bhagavad Gītā — 18 Life Lessons, 700 Verses, 8 Commentary Traditions | The Bhagavad Gītā
URL: https://bhagavad.net

The Bhagavad Gītā 18 life lessons from a warrior in crisis and his divine friend. 700 verses · 8 commentary traditions · One search box. Read as a book → Download free PDF 01 Inner conflict is the starting point of all growth Arjuna's Dilemma · 47 verses 02 Stop identifying with what changes The Path of Knowledge · 72 verses 03 Act without obsessing over results The Path of Action · 43 verses 04 Knowledge burns karma like fire burns fuel Knowledge & Renunciation · 42 verses 05 Be free while fully engaged in the world Renunciation of Action · 29 verses 06 The mind is your greatest ally or worst enemy The Path of Meditation · 47 verses 07 One reality in infinite forms Knowledge & Realization · 30 verses 08 Your last thought shapes what comes next The Imperishable Absolute · 28 verses 09 A leaf offered with love reaches the divine Royal Knowledge · 34 verses 10 Everything extraordinary is a glimpse of the infinite Divine Manifestations · 42 verses 11 Reality is vaster than the mind can hold The Cosmic Vision · 55 verses 12 Love is the fastest path The Path of Devotion · 20 verses 13 You are the awareness, not the body The Field & The Knower · 34 verses 14 Clarity, passion, inertia — name them, then rise above The Three Gunas · 27 verses 15 Cut the tree of attachment with detachment The Supreme Person · 20 verses 16 Desire, anger, greed — three gates to self-destruction Divine & Demonic Natures · 24 verses 17 Your faith shapes what you eat, give, and become Three Kinds of Faith · 28 verses 18 Surrender everything — then act with full freedom Liberation Through Surrender · 78 verses 8 Commentary Traditions Advaita, Dvaita, Vishishtadvaita, Bhakti, Kashmir Shaivism, Shuddhadvaita, Karma Yoga, Practical Vedanta. 4 Life Pillars Personal growth, career, health, and relationships — each verse mapped with reflection questions. 14 Day Sanskrit Course Start the course — read Devanagari and IAST from scratch.
